name: Driver: San Francisco
post_apply_message:
Driver San Francisco — three fixes applied:
1. Intro Bink files (DriverSplash + ubisoft) renamed to .gfm-orig
2. DXVK's d3d9.dll dropped next to Driver.exe (fixes the SteamOS story-mode crash-to-desktop)
3. Launch wrapper installed at ~/gfm-wrappers/dsf-launch.sh — automatically bypasses the dead Ubisoft Game Launcher and runs Driver.exe directly. PCGW confirms the game is fully self-sufficient this way. Ubisoft Connect servers died Oct 2022, so DLC/multiplayer is offline-only regardless; single-player works fine.
notes: Delisted from Steam Dec 2016, Ubisoft Connect online services shut down Oct 2022 — but the Steam single-player installs still work. Three legit fixes bundled: (1) intro skip per PCGW; (2) DXVK d3d9.dll drop is PCGW's documented Linux/SteamOS fix for story-mode CTD (the bundled Ubisoft d3d9 is buggy under Proton, DXVK's shim overrides it); (3) launcher-bypass wrapper (same waitforexitandrun-swap pattern as our TWFC wrapper) — reads %command%, replaces the last .exe arg with Driver.exe (tries both cases), execs the modified proton stack. PCGW confirms 'Steam not required when launched from the executable on Windows' — Ubisoft launcher's auth check just delays boot under dead servers. DXVK v3.0.1 pinned (later versions may have D3D9 regressions with old games). No SilentPatch for this game exists yet.
